spec_match.rationale: The capability probe is copy-pasted boilerplate that checks unrelated features (navigator.gpu, navigator.mediaDevices, navigator.credentials) instead of CSS.supports('flex-wrap', 'balance').

interactive.rationale: Highly static comparison. There are no card resize handle controls, text editors, or tag adder inputs, and the A/B comparisons are fully hardcoded.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The card container concept matches the target use case, showing how labels wrap on grid articles.

edge_cases.rationale: Fails to test dynamic limits, extreme label counts, or differing viewport breakpoints.

references.rationale: Correctly cites the primary ChromeStatus milestones page.

design_system.rationale: Beautiful layout, with clear division panels showing wrap vs balance configurations.

openQuestions.title: Fix irrelevant capability checks in live probe
openQuestions.detail: The capability probe currently queries navigator.gpu, navigator.credentials, and navigator.mediaDevices. Replace these boilerplate checks with a direct capability test for CSS.supports('flex-wrap', 'balance') and update the readout to explain CSS engine compatibility.

openQuestions.title: Add card resizing or card tag editing controls
openQuestions.detail: The cards are currently static. Add a card width range slider or inline editable text blocks so developers can test wrap behavior under variable spacing.

summary: The headline-equalizer renders beautiful layouts but has low interactivity and contains copy-pasted boilerplate in its live probe checking unrelated APIs.
